title: Engineering the Process — Standard Operating Procedures
subtitle: Policy, Procedure, and Technical Work Discipline

1.0.name: Purpose
1.0.description: Intent of the document; 1-2 sentences maximum

2.0.name: Scope
2.0.description: Who and what the procedure applies to; explicitly states what is NOT in scope

3.0.name: References
3.0.description: All documents, standards, and tools needed to understand and execute the procedure

4.0.name: Definitions
4.0.description: Terms that may not be universally understood; acronyms; context-specific meanings

5.0.name: Roles & Responsibilities
5.0.description: Who performs, who reviews, who approves. RACI matrix preferred

6.0.name: Procedure
6.0.description: Step-by-step instructions in execution order. Measurable criteria in steps

7.0.name: Quality Checks
7.0.description: Inspection points, critical dimensions, and verification steps

8.0.name: Records / Success Criteria
8.0.description: Evidence the procedure was followed and succeeded. Audit-ready outputs

skill_md_mapping.2.0_Scope: When to Use / When NOT to Use

government.title: NASA Procedural Requirements 7150.2D: Software Engineering Requirements
NASA Software Assurance and Software Safety Standard

standards.title: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15289:2017 Content of life-cycle information items
IEEE Std 829-2008 Software and System Test Documentation
IEEE Std 1028 Software Reviews and Audits

research.title: Software Process Improvement and Product Line Practice
SOP-Agent: Empower General Purpose AI Agent with Domain-Specific SOPs
How to Write Effective SOPs for Systems Engineering
Standard Operating Procedure: Full Implementation Guide
AI-Powered SOPs: How Modern Teams Document and Train Faster

success_criteria.description: Complete SOP anatomy template covering all 8 canonical sections with worked examples
CMMI 5-level mapping to observable team behaviors with improvement paths
Policy hierarchy framework: org-wide, project-level, task-level relationships
SOP lifecycle model with explicit gates, roles, version-control requirements
AI-augmented SOP patterns catalogued with human review guidance
DACP documented as concrete specification for agent-executable procedures
SKILL.md formally characterized as bounded SOP with anatomy mapping
Maturity self-assessment tool with CMMI observable indicators
Change-control workflow: triggers, review roles, version bump rules
NASA NPR 7150.2D compliance mapped to GSD wave-plan equivalents
MV SOP template validated against 3 existing GSD skills
3+ cross-module architectural patterns unifying SOP + GSD design

FLIGHT.role: Mission orchestration; M5 synthesis; go/no-go gates

EXEC_A.role: M1 SOP Anatomy + M2 Process Maturity production

CRAFT_PROC.role: CMMI depth; NASA SE methodology; maturity assessment

CRAFT_GOV.role: Policy hierarchy; lifecycle modeling; version-control SOP

VERIFY.role: Source validation; accuracy checking; criteria verification

CAPCOM.role: Wave boundary approval; synthesis review; final gate

SURGEON.role: Research quality monitoring; coverage gap alerts

LOG.role: Commit messages; audit trail; status tracking
